mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (interaction term coefficient insignificant at p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production reflect supply-side contraction and capacity utilization drops, forcing equity markets lower over the next month during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low policy rates combined with rising nonfarm payrolls create a liquidity-driven risk-on environment, generating strong positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal of low FEDFUNDS and rising PAYEMS yields negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly translates to stronger corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as macro fundamentals improve.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across all market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls surge above 200,000 signals robust labor demand and wage growth, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS surges above 200k are followed by negative 20-day returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside stagnant industrial production indicate stagflationary pressures, compressing profit margins and forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of high CPIAUCSL and flat INDPRO fails to predict negative 60-day returns (Rank IC > 0.02).
